name: American Tourister Moonlight Hardside Carry-On Luggage
whatWorks: At under $100 CAD, this is among the lowest-priced hardshell spinner carry-ons that actually meets major airline overhead bin dimensions—budget travelers get a functional, TSA-locked bag without overpaying
The four-wheel spinner rolls smoothly on airport floors and can be pushed one-handed alongside you without tipping, a significant comfort advantage over two-wheel rollers on long terminal walks
The built-in TSA combination lock saves the cost of purchasing a separate lock and reduces the risk of TSA cutting off an aftermarket lock during inspection
ABS hardshell protects fragile contents—cameras, gifts, souvenirs—better than softside bags at the same price point
worthKnowing: ABS plastic dents and scratches more visibly than polycarbonate; expect cosmetic marks after checked-bag-rough handling, though the structural integrity holds up well
At 6.4 lbs empty, this is on the heavier side for a carry-on—Travelpro's Maxlite 5 softside saves nearly 1.5 lbs, which matters when airlines are strict about 10 kg carry-on limits
The interior is split-panel with a cross-strap divider only; no dedicated pockets or organizer panels mean everything sits in two flat halves

commonComplaints.title: Scratches and scuffs easily
commonComplaints.body: The shiny ABS finish shows scratches prominently after a single checked-bag flight. Travelers who care about luggage appearance quickly regret a light color; matte finishes or dark colors fare somewhat better.

commonComplaints.title: Zipper pull feels lightweight
commonComplaints.body: The main zipper pull is a small, lightweight piece of plastic. Several reviewers report it breaking off within the first year of heavy use, requiring a key ring as a replacement or a warranty claim.

commonComplaints.title: Interior lacks organization pockets
commonComplaints.body: No divider pockets or compression straps inside beyond the two main panels. Clothes shift during transit, and small items like chargers or toiletries need their own bags to stay organized.

verdict: The American Tourister Moonlight is the right carry-on for first-time travelers or budget-conscious flyers who want a hardshell spinner with a TSA lock and don't need premium durability. It handles 10–20 trips before showing meaningful wear, which suits the occasional vacation traveler well. If you fly monthly or need a bag that stays pristine, spend up to Samsonite Omni 2 or Travelpro territory—but for once-or-twice-a-year travel, this delivers everything you need for under $100.

realBuyers.quote: Used this for four round trips over two years and it's still going strong. Has some scratches but nothing structural. For the price I'm completely satisfied.
Perfect first luggage purchase. Fits in every overhead bin I've tried (Air Canada, WestJet, Porter) and the spinner wheels are genuinely smooth.
The TSA lock is a nice bonus but the shiny finish shows every mark. Buy it in black if you care about looks.

metaDescription: American Tourister Moonlight carry-on review: best budget hardshell spinner under $100 CAD. TSA lock, 4-wheel spinner, fits airline overhead bins.
introText: The American Tourister Moonlight Hardside Carry-On (ASIN B01N8SXKLF) is a 21-inch ABS hardshell spinner with a built-in TSA lock, available on Amazon.ca for under $100. It offers four-wheel spinning convenience and 2-inch expansion capacity at an entry-level price point.
